title: HAL remote teleport: selecting old conversation from HistoryView leaves HAL overlay stuck (Exit no-op)
description:
After a successful HAL teleport to remote runtime, a new remote conversation starts (good; begins with teleport summary).

Bug: If the user opens HistoryView and selects an older conversation (pre-teleport / local conversation), the HAL overlay appears/stays visible and becomes stuck. The Exit button does not work, leaving the UI unusable.

Observed (2026-01-13):
- Teleport succeeds; connected to remote server.
- Open HistoryView, pick an old conversation.
- HAL overlay shows "Restricted Area Protocol" and Exit button, but clicking Exit does nothing; overlay remains.

Screenshot: 2026-01-13 02:44:43.

Hypothesis:
- HAL state machine doesn’t reset/unsuppress on conversation restore or mode switch, or ignores exit/cancel messages after history selection changes conversation context.
acceptance_criteria:
- Repro reliably and identify which message/state transition is missing (history restore vs teleport state).
- Ensure selecting a conversation from HistoryView always leaves the UI in a usable state (HAL overlay hidden or Exit works).
- Add a regression test (webview unit test) if feasible.

title: ask_oracle tool: query a high-quality LLM for second opinion
description:
Inspiration: AMP’s ‘oracle’ pattern — a separate, very-strong LLM (e.g., Anthropic Claude Opus 4.5) that the agent can consult when stuck or needing a second opinion on complex questions.

Goal: Add an ask_oracle tool the agent can call to query an external ‘oracle’ LLM.

Tool contract:
- name: ask_oracle
- description: Ask a dedicated oracle LLM a question when you are unsure or want a second opinion. You MUST include relevant context in your question so the oracle can answer correctly (e.g., code snippets, markdown explanations, or diagrams in Mermaid if helpful).
- input schema:
  - question: string (required) — the exact question to ask the oracle.
  - context: string (optional) — additional detail the agent assembles to provide sufficient background.
- output: string (the oracle’s answer). Consider returning a structured envelope with { answer, citations? } later; start simple with plaintext answer.

Settings:
- Add a setting to select the oracle LLM profile by id (e.g., openhands.oracle.profileId). Reuse the existing LLM Profiles store. If unset, tool returns an instructive error and suggests configuring the oracle profile.

Runtime behavior:
- On tool call, resolve the oracle LLM client from the selected profile.
- Build a concise system prompt like: ‘You are an Oracle. Provide a careful, direct answer. If you are uncertain, say so and suggest next steps. Be concise unless the question asks for detail.’
- Construct the user message using input.question and append the provided context (clearly delimited) to ensure the oracle has sufficient background.
- Respect token limits; truncate overly large context sensibly (optional follow-up).

Tests (unit; no integration needed):
- Mock the oracle LLM client and verify:
  - Tool validates required ‘question’ and accepts optional ‘context’.
  - It builds the prompt with an <environment/context> style delimiter (or equivalent) and passes it to the LLM.
  - It returns the mocked answer plain-text to the observation.
  - When oracle profile is missing/misconfigured, the tool returns a friendly error prompting to set openhands.oracle.profileId.
- Cover edge cases: empty/whitespace question (reject), large context (truncate), non-string inputs (schema validation).

Notes:
- Future: consider structured output, references, and cost/usage telemetry.
- UI: Settings pane entry for selecting the oracle profile from existing profiles.
acceptance_criteria:
- New ask_oracle tool available in @openhands/agent-sdk-ts tools package with JSON schema { question: string (required), context: string (optional) }.
- Tool reads oracle LLM profileId from settings (e.g., openhands.oracle.profileId). If unset, returns an instructive error.
- Tool invokes the configured LLM and returns the oracle’s answer as the observation result.
- Unit tests thoroughly cover success and error paths using a mocked LLM client. No integration tests are required.
- Clear tool description instructs the agent to include sufficient context (code snippets, markdown, diagrams) when calling the tool.

title: Hide environment info from Webview in ConversationView
description:
Problem:
Environment information is being displayed in the ConversationView webview alongside the user's message, e.g.:

"do you see the opened file?

<environment information> Active editor: README.md Open editors: - README.md </environment information>
"

This is surprising and should not be visible to the user.

Scope:
- Continue sending environment info to the LLM (for context) but do not render it in the webview message timeline.

Repro:
1. Open the OpenHands Tab extension.
2. Start a conversation and send a message while an editor is open.
3. Observe the user message rendered in the webview includes the <environment information> block.

Notes:
- For now, suppress rendering in the webview only. Keep the payload for the LLM unchanged.
- Adjust ConversationView message rendering to strip or hide this metadata.
acceptance_criteria:
- Environment info blocks are never rendered in ConversationView (for both user and agent messages).
- Environment info still reaches the LLM unchanged.
- Existing conversations no longer show the environment block after reload.
- Add a simple unit/e2e assertion to prevent regressions (optional if infra exists).

title: Teleport decision should auto-reject local pending tool call with explicit reason
description:
Spec clarification: when HAL decision ends with "Teleport to Remote", the *local* conversation must record an automatic rejection of the pending tool call (as a normal user rejection) with reason: "rejected because the user sent the conversation to the remote runtime". This ensures restoring the old conversation from HistoryView is usable and consistent.

Current behavior:
- Teleport command in `src/hal/registerHalCommands.ts` calls `deps.getConversation()?.rejectAction("Teleported to remote runtime")` after switching connection; may reject on the wrong conversation instance and uses different reason string.

Expected:
- Reject is applied to the original local conversation pending confirmation (same tool_call_id) once remote connection is established.
- Rejection reason matches the explicit message.

Acceptance:
- Restoring the pre-teleport conversation shows the rejection event/tool message and does NOT trigger HAL overlay.
- Tests cover rejecting local vs remote conversation objects.

- [ ] Regression tests added
- [ ] Related edge cases tested

title: Investigation: Switch conversation workspace folder mid-stream; PRD update
description:
Question: How can we switch the agent in an existing conversation (and at start) to work on a different VS Code workspaceFolder than the first one? Capture findings and propose UX/tech approaches; do not implement.

Scope of investigation:
- Current behavior audit: code paths that assume workspaceFolders[0] for workspaceRoot (extension.ts, webview host helpers, settings adapter, git diff, history paths).
- Constraints: agent local mode constructs LocalWorkspace with a workspaceRoot; remote mode may pass workspaceRoot via API or be server-defined.
- Options to consider:
  1) Per-conversation workspaceRoot selection (UI in the chat header) that re-instantiates LocalConversation bound to a selected workspace folder.
  2) Command palette command to switch the workspace folder for the active conversation; persists with conversation state.
  3) Multi-root awareness: store a workspaceFolder index/identifier and use it consistently in all code paths (paths, diffs, openFile, globs).
  4) Remote mode parity: include workspaceRoot (or relative root) in Start Conversation payload; server-side implications.
- Edge cases: files outside any workspace folder; folder removed/renamed; diff/git-root resolution; restoring conversations after reload.

Deliverable:
- Update PRD with the recommended approach, UX touchpoints, and technical changes across modules (startup, message handler, local tools, settings persistence). Include trade-offs and risks.
- Timebox a simple prototype plan if applicable (not to be implemented in this bead).

Testing strategy (to outline in PRD):
- Unit tests for workspace selection logic, path resolution and persistence.
- E2E test flow to switch workspace and verify file/diff opens, tools operate under the new root.
acceptance_criteria:
- PRD updated with a dedicated section describing how to switch the conversation’s workspaceFolder mid-stream and at start.
- The doc lists: current assumptions, proposed designs (with pros/cons), remote-mode considerations, and testing approach.
- No code changes in this bead; outcome is documentation only.

title: Conversation persistence location: use ~/.openhands/conversations-vscode
description:
Investigate where OpenHands-Tab saves conversation base JSON and events `events.jsonl` (and any other persisted conversation state).

Requirement:
- Persist under `~/.openhands/conversations-vscode`.
- If anything is currently being persisted under the workspace directory (e.g. `./.openhands/...` inside the repo), move it to the persistence dir above.

Code pointers / suspects:
- Extension local-mode already resolves a store root via `src/extension/conversationStoreRoot.ts` and prefers `~/.openhands/conversations-vscode` (fallbacks: VS Code globalStorageUri + os.tmpdir).
- However, agent-sdk-ts FileStore default root dir is currently `path.join(process.cwd(), ".openhands", "conversations")` (see `packages/agent-sdk-ts/src/sdk/runtime/persistence.ts`). In any codepath where `persistenceDir` is not explicitly passed, this will write inside the repo/workspace.

Tasks:
1) Audit all call sites that construct `Conversation`/`LocalConversation` and ensure `persistenceDir` is always set to the desired root for the VS Code extension.
2) Ensure no other persistence layer writes conversation artifacts into the workspace.
3) If needed, change agent-sdk-ts default persistence root from `process.cwd()` to `os.homedir()` (or make it accept an explicit default injected by the host), so standalone usage does not write into the repo.

Notes:
- Keep the existing `openhands.conversation.storeRoot` setting as an override, but default should be stable and homedir-based.
- Consider migration strategy if files already exist in the workspace path.
acceptance_criteria:
- All conversation base JSON and events JSONL are written under `~/.openhands/conversations-vscode` by default.
- No conversation persistence files are written inside the workspace/repo directory unless the user explicitly configures `openhands.conversation.storeRoot` to do so.
- Existing workspace persistence (if found) is migrated or clearly deprecated with a one-time move + log message.
- Tests updated accordingly.

title: Teleport: remote convo starts without summary (asks for events)
description:
Observed after teleporting to remote runtime: the remote conversation is started with a message like:

"Teleported from the local VS Code runtime after a HIGH-risk confirmation.
Repo: oh-tab
Branch: release/v0.5-workflow
Remote: git@github.com:enyst/OpenHands-Tab.git
Note: uncommitted local changes may not be present remotely.

---

Please provide the list of events for me to summarize. I am ready to apply the rules"

Expected:
- During teleport, we should run a summarizer with a non-empty set of recent local events, and then start the *remote* conversation with an actual summary text (not a request for events).

Suspected regression / code pointers:
- Teleport summary is implemented in `src/hal/registerHalCommands.ts` (teleport command): it calls `deps.ensureConversationAndConnection(...)` (switching to remote) **before** building the summary events list via `deps.iterConversationEventBacklog()`. If `iterConversationEventBacklog()` is bound to the *current* conversation, it may now point at the new remote conversation (empty backlog), resulting in an empty prompt and the LLM responding with a request for events.
- Fix likely involves capturing the local backlog events (and/or local ConversationInstance) *before* switching to remote, then using those captured events to build the summarization prompt.
- Preferred profile: `gemini-flash-summarizer` (same as tool summaries), **but** if the user has no Gemini API key configured, fall back to the main agent LLM profile for this teleport/HAL summarization.

Notes:
- This used to work; likely impacted by recent HAL/teleport changes.
acceptance_criteria:
- Teleport always generates a summary during the teleport flow and the remote conversation starts with the generated summary text.
- Summary input includes recent local Action/Observation/Message events (non-empty in normal use).
- Summary generation prefers `gemini-flash-summarizer`; if the Gemini API key is not configured, it falls back to the main agent LLM profile (does not fail or ask for events).
- If summarization fails **or returns empty text**, remote message includes a clearly labeled fallback with the **last 10** teleportable events.
- Documentation updated: `docs/hal/elevenlabs_prd.md` documents this teleport summarization behavior (profile selection + fallback last-10-events + non-empty guarantee).
notes: - Confirmed: `docs/hal/elevenlabs_prd.md` already documents the “summarization fails → include last 10 events” fallback. Keep it, and extend docs to also cover (a) Gemini-key-missing fallback to main agent LLM, and (b) guardrail against empty summary output.

title: Python parity: ThinkTool missing in agent-sdk-ts
description:
Python agent-sdk reportedly includes a ThinkTool (often used for structured reasoning / planning-only steps), but @openhands/agent-sdk-ts does not implement or expose an equivalent tool.

This is a parity gap between SDKs and may confuse users who expect the same default tool surface across Python and VS Code local mode.

Also: check our parity guide and confirm whether it documents this gap; if it does not, update it.
acceptance_criteria:
- Decide whether agent-sdk-ts should implement ThinkTool (preferred) or explicitly omit it.
- If implemented: add ThinkTool to agent-sdk-ts (schema + execution semantics), ensure it is included/handled consistently in tool lists/UI where applicable, and add unit tests.
- Update packages/agent-sdk-ts/docs/python-parity.md to mention ThinkTool parity status (and rationale if omitted).
- Ensure any changes keep tool-count/logging consistent between UI and actual LLM request tools.

title: Conversation webview Tools badge count misses 'finish'
description:
In Conversation webview, the bottom-row Tools badge shows Tools(3), but the extension log indicates 4 tools are being sent to the model, including 'finish'.

Observed:
- Webview UI: Tools(3) (superscript on Tools label).
- After sending 'hi' to the agent, logs show: "2026-01-12 19:40:02.947 [info] [llm] Sending request to gpt-5 with tools (4): terminal, file_editor, task_tracker, finish".

Expected:
- UI should reflect the actual enabled/sent tool set (count + names), including 'finish' when enabled.

Hypothesis:
- Two sources of truth exist (e.g., UI derives enabled tools from one config/state, while LLM request builder derives from another). We should deduplicate so there is a single canonical enabled-tools list that both UI and LLM request use.
acceptance_criteria: Tools count/name list in Conversation webview matches the tools passed in LLM requests; 'finish' is shown (or consistently omitted everywhere if intentionally implicit). Enabled-tools source of truth is unified (no divergence between UI and request builder).

title: Docs: HAL 9000 easter egg naming alignment
description:
User-facing naming should use 'HAL 9000 easter egg' consistently.

Scope:
- Update docs/settings_prd.md section title currently 'HAL (high-risk confirmation flow)'.
- Scan docs/src for other headings/labels where this feature name is described and align (docs-only unless there is a trivial UI label mismatch).

Do NOT touch deferred issues/PRs (#667/#625/#570).
acceptance_criteria:
- `rg "HAL (high-risk confirmation flow)"` returns no matches.
- docs/settings_prd.md uses 'HAL 9000 easter egg' in the section title (keep the parenthetical '(high-risk confirmation flow)' if helpful).
- No behavior changes.
notes: BlackDog: docs naming aligned to 'HAL 9000 easter egg'. PR #759.

title: E2E: HAL does not trigger on non-HIGH risk / disabled
description:
Add hermetic E2E coverage for negative HAL cases so we do not regress behavior.

Suggested assertions (no DOM automation):
- With openhands.hal.enabled=true and confirmation policy set, send an ActionEvent with security_risk=MEDIUM and WAITING_FOR_CONFIRMATION. Assert openhands._queryHalState().phase stays idle.
- With openhands.hal.enabled=false, send an ActionEvent with security_risk=HIGH and WAITING_FOR_CONFIRMATION. Assert openhands._queryHalState().phase stays idle.

Implementation notes:
- Reuse existing commands/helpers: openhands._sendTestEvent, openhands._queryHalState, pollUntil.
- Keep it isolated from network/audio; do not require external API keys.

Do NOT touch deferred issues/PRs (#667/#625/#570).
acceptance_criteria:
- New E2E test(s) cover the two negative cases above.
- npm run e2e passes locally.
- No changes to production behavior (test-only).
notes: BlackDog: implemented HAL negative-case E2E suite (halNegative). PR #758.

title: Remote mode: Tools picker missing from bottom row
description:
In remote mode (connected to agent-server after HAL teleport), the bottom accessory row shows LLM Profile selector and Skills, but Tools control is missing.

Expected/Question:
- Should remote mode also show a Tools button + dropdown (like local mode), or is Tools selection intentionally local-only?
- If remote supports a tool list (server-reported), we likely want to show it for transparency even if not user-configurable mid-conversation.

Screenshot: 2026-01-13 02:24 (teleport success).

Notes:
- Current local mode shows Tools + count and supports enabling/disabling tools pre-conversation.
- Remote agent-server exposes a tools list endpoint, but tool selection semantics may differ.
acceptance_criteria:
- Determine intended UX for Tools in remote mode (show/hide, read-only vs configurable).
- If Tools should be visible: add UI to display remote tool list/count (and optionally disable toggling).
- If Tools should remain hidden: document rationale in code/docs so users aren’t surprised.
notes:
Additional observed detail after remote teleport (2026-01-13 02:36):
- Under the System Prompt event block, the Tools list renders as generic placeholders (tool_1 .. tool_5) instead of real tool names.
- Suggests remote-mode SystemPromptEvent tools payload may be missing names/function names, or the webview tool-name extraction is failing for server-provided tool schemas.

title: Debug LLM_REQUEST log should truncate reasoning.encrypted_content
description:
In OpenHands-DEBUG OUTPUT, the `[LLM_REQUEST]` log prints the full `encrypted_content` from a `responses_reasoning_item`.

Problem:
- `encrypted_content` is long and unreadable (encrypted blob), and makes the debug view noisy.

Request:
- Truncate `encrypted_content` for display ONLY (do not mutate the actual request object/value sent to the LLM).
- Suggested format: first 4 chars + `…` + last 4 chars (preserve ability to visually verify prefix/suffix and that it is populated).

Acceptance criteria:
- In `[LLM_REQUEST]` debug output, `encrypted_content` is rendered truncated.
- The underlying request payload remains unchanged (full value still sent; truncation is display-layer only).

title: Tool result tooltip overlaps tool name
description:
In the Conversation webview, hovering the control shows a tooltip ('Show tool result') that opens to the left and covers/obscures the tool name text (e.g., 'file_editor'). This makes the tool label unreadable while the tooltip is visible.

Expected:
- Tooltip placement should not cover the tool name; in this UI it should open on the right side (or otherwise flip/offset to avoid obscuring adjacent text).

Repro:
- Hover the tool-result toggle/control in the tool result header; observe tooltip covers the tool name (see screenshot from 2026-01-12 19:44:35).
acceptance_criteria: Tooltip does not obscure the tool name text while visible (e.g., 'file_editor' stays readable). Placement defaults to right-side (or uses auto-flip/avoid-collision) with appropriate spacing.

title: HAL teleport: looping music + Exit disabled + slow failure when server down
description:
When choosing 'Teleport to Remote' from the HAL overlay, the UI enters waiting_remote and plays the bundled music sting in a loop. If the first configured server is down (e.g. workspace config has http://localhost:3000 but agent-server isn't running), the underlying RemoteConversation connect/retry can take a long time, so the audio loops indefinitely.

Additionally, the HAL overlay Exit button was disabled while teleport was in progress, so users couldn't cancel/return to the normal confirmation UI.

Suggested fix:
- Keep Exit enabled during waiting_remote and make it cancel teleport + stop audio + return to confirmation.
- Add a fast /health liveness check before switching to remote to avoid long retry loops when server is down.
- Optionally ignore late halTeleport* host messages after user exits.
acceptance_criteria: During HAL waiting_remote, Exit is clickable and stops audio, cancels teleport, and returns to normal confirmation UI. If server is down, teleport fails quickly (health check) instead of looping audio for an extended period.

title: Docs: document openhands.oracle.profileId
description: Document the new OpenHands setting openhands.oracle.profileId in docs/PRD.md and docs/settings_prd.md so users discover the oracle profile selector.
acceptance_criteria:
- docs/PRD.md mentions openhands.oracle.profileId in the Settings section.
- docs/settings_prd.md documents openhands.oracle.profileId under LLM settings.
notes: OrangeCastle taking as tech-debt while BlackDog works oh-tab-ox2

title: Fix: ask_oracle context truncation limit
description: CI currently fails in AskOracleTool truncation test due to mismatch between MAX_CONTEXT_CHARS and test inputs/expectations. Align implementation + test so overly-large context is truncated and the user message stays within ~60k chars.
acceptance_criteria:
- AskOracleTool truncates large context and inserts <context clipped> marker.
- Unit test for truncation reliably triggers and passes.
- CI passes.
notes: OrangeCastle: fix CI break uncovered by PR #749

title: E2E: ask_oracle uses configured oracle profile
description: Add a hermetic E2E that sets openhands.oracle.profileId to a mock LLM profile and verifies: (1) the main LLM can request the ask_oracle tool via tool_calls, (2) ask_oracle makes a second request using the configured oracle profile (to a different mock server), and (3) the tool observation returns the oracle server’s mocked answer.
acceptance_criteria:
- New E2E test verifies ask_oracle success-path with configured openhands.oracle.profileId.
- Test is hermetic (uses local mock servers; no external network).
- Test asserts the oracle server is called exactly once and the observation contains the oracle answer.

title: Tech debt: dedupe E2E OpenAI tool-calls mock server
description: Refactor the duplicated OpenAI chat_completions SSE tool_calls mock server code used by tests/e2e/suite/oracleUnset.ts and tests/e2e/suite/oracleConfigured.ts into a shared helper under tests/e2e/suite/helpers/. No behavior changes.
acceptance_criteria:
- Shared helper exists for the OpenAI tool_calls SSE mock.
- oracleUnset and oracleConfigured suites use the helper.
- CI (unit + e2e) remains green.

title: HistoryView: tokens row + titles + prompt clamp
description:
HistoryView is currently visually bland and not very informative (see the screenshot from 2026-01-13 03:46).

Requested improvements:
- Under the relative time (e.g. “56m ago”), add a row showing per-conversation context token usage: `Context: <n> tokens` (same value/source as the Header totals row for the active ConversationView).
- Conversation title: display a real title if we have one; if not, generate one (max 7 words) using the same Gemini-flash setup used for tool summaries, persist it into the conversation base JSON, and load it for HistoryView.
- Prompt preview: clamp the displayed first user prompt to a maximum of 5 lines (not just a character limit).

Notes:
- Title generation should be cached/persisted so HistoryView opening does not re-request titles for previously-titled conversations.
- If title generation fails/unavailable, fall back to the current title heuristic.
acceptance_criteria:
- History list items show `Context: <n> tokens` under the time-ago for each conversation.
- If a persisted title exists, it is used; titles are max 7 words.
- If no title exists, the app generates one via Gemini-flash and persists it to the conversation base JSON; subsequent HistoryView opens reuse it.
- First user prompt preview is visually clamped to 5 lines.
- No UX regression: HistoryView search/filter and selection still behave as before.

title: Unit: tighten ask_oracle context truncation assertion
description: After merging oh-tab-ox2, AskOracleTool truncation unit test assertion was loosened. Restore the stricter expectation to ensure user message stays under ~60k chars when truncating context.
acceptance_criteria: - AskOracleTool truncation unit test asserts the final user message length stays under ~60k chars and contains <context clipped>.

title: Tests: file-editor observations render filename as VS Code diff link
description:
Add tests to verify that when the agent edits a file via file_editor (insert/str_replace/create), the filename shown in the Conversation view is a clickable link that opens a VS Code diff view.

Scope:
- Preferred: E2E test that drives the webview and verifies message handling path:
  - Simulate ObservationEvent for file_editor with old_content/new_content and path.
  - Ensure the webview renders an InlineFileDiffReference button.
  - Simulate a click and assert the extension host receives openWorkspaceDiff and calls showWorkspaceDiff (or vscode.diff) with correct arguments (path, content, preferGitHead=true).
- If E2E is too heavy for CI, add unit tests:
  - Webview unit: Event rendering creates a button that posts { type: 'openWorkspaceDiff', ... }.
  - Host unit: createWebviewMessageHandler handles 'openWorkspaceDiff' by calling showWorkspaceDiff and respects preferGitHead.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Unit tests pass for both webview component and host message handler.
- If e2e is feasible, at least one test validates the click → diff command flow end-to-end.
- Coverage includes at least one 'insert' or 'str_replace' case and the 'create' case.
- Tests assert that the preferGitHead option is passed along when present.

Notes:
- Relevant code: src/webview-src/components/eventBlocks/shared.tsx (InlineFileDiffReference/openWorkspaceDiff), src/webview/host/createWebviewMessageHandler.ts (openWorkspaceDiff), src/webview/host/diffDocuments.ts, src/webview/host/gitHeadDiff.ts.
acceptance_criteria:
- Rendering: Given a file_editor ObservationEvent with path + old_content + new_content, the Conversation view shows a clickable diff UI element.
- Webview → Host: Clicking the element posts an openWorkspaceDiff message with the expected payload.
- Host behavior: The handler resolves the diff contents (preferring Git HEAD when requested) and opens a VS Code diff (showWorkspaceDiff or vscode.diff) with correct labels.
- At least one test covers 'insert' or 'str_replace' and one covers 'create'.

title: Tool risk tooltip text too long (causes horizontal scrollbar)
description:
In the tool-call UI, the tooltip above the SecurityRiskBadge (“risk” label) is too long and can force an unwanted horizontal scrollbar.

Requested changes (text-only; no layout changes):
- Change tooltip prefix from “The model assessed …” → “The agent assessed …”.
- Truncate the message to end at “risk.” (remove trailing “for this action” / anything after “risk”).

Current code:
- `src/webview-src/components/SecurityRiskBadge.tsx` has: `The model assessed ${risk.toLowerCase()} risk for this action.`

Acceptance:
- Tooltip reads exactly: `The agent assessed <low|medium|high> risk.`
- No more horizontal scrollbar introduced by this tooltip text.

title: Rename label: 'Tool Result' → 'Environment Result' (webview display only)
description:
Change the displayed header text in the Conversation view from 'Tool Result' to 'Environment Result'.

Scope:
- Webview only; no behavioral changes.
- Affects ObservationEventBlock header labels (both generic and Finish tool special-case header) where it currently shows 'Tool Result'.
- Ensure no regression in tests relying on the text (update snapshots if needed).

Rationale: Better conveys that the block represents results coming from the environment/tool execution, not just 'tools' in the abstract.
acceptance_criteria:
- All Conversation view blocks that previously displayed 'Tool Result' now display 'Environment Result'.
- No change to functionality or styling.
- Unit tests updated accordingly (if they assert the label).

title: Parity check: ThinkTool matches Python agent-sdk (description/definition/behavior)
description:
Review ~/repos/agent-sdk (Python original) ThinkTool and ensure @openhands/agent-sdk-ts ThinkTool has the same tool name, description, input schema, and intended behavior so that LLM requests are aligned.

Checklist:
- Tool name should be 'think'.
- Description text and guidance should match Python’s (or deliberately equivalent) so LLM knows to ‘log a thought without side effects’. Consider any nuance (e.g., brainstorming/planning wording).
- Input schema: a single required field (e.g., 'thought': string); confirm field name and description match Python’s to minimize prompt drift.
- Behavior: no side effects; return a simple confirmation message. Ensure the result is properly captured in observations and included in context for the next turn.

Deliverables:
- If differences exist, propose minimal changes to TS ThinkTool to reach parity and update tests.
- Update parity doc (packages/agent-sdk-ts/docs/python-parity.md) with the outcome.

Notes:
- This is a parity investigation + small change task; if substantial changes are needed, follow up with a separate implementation bead.
acceptance_criteria:
- Documented comparison between Python and TS ThinkTool with name/description/schema/behavior.
- If mismatches are found, a proposed change list is included; minor changes can be implemented in a follow-up PR.
- Tests updated or confirmed to ensure ThinkTool observations are persisted and fed into next-turn context.

title: gpt-5 reasoningSummary=detailed yields empty reasoning summary
description:
User set LLM Profile "gpt-5" -> "reasoning summary" = "detailed", but OpenHands-DEBUG OUTPUT logs show the model output reasoning summary is empty.

Observed (OpenHands-DEBUG OUTPUT channel, ActionEvent):
- `responses_reasoning_item.summary` is `[]`
- `responses_reasoning_item.content` is `null`
- `thought` and `thinking_blocks` are also `[]`

Example log snippet:
```
[2026-01-13 01:02:59.052] [EVENT_ActionEvent]
{
  "responses_reasoning_item": {
    "id": "rs_0875...",
    "summary": [],
    "content": null
  },
  ...
}
```

We also appear to be sending the correct param to the model:
- Request log shows `"parameters":{"reasoningSummary":"detailed"}` with tools `["terminal","file_editor","finish"]`.

Expected:
- When `reasoningSummary` is set to `detailed`, the response should include a non-empty reasoning summary (per OpenAI Responses API reasoning item format).

Questions:
- Are we parsing the Responses API reasoning output item correctly?
- Is `reasoningSummary` being applied to the correct request field for `gpt-5`?
- Is the model returning an empty summary for some reason and we need fallback/telemetry?

title: E2E: ask_oracle tool error path when unset
description: Add an E2E test that triggers an ask_oracle tool call while openhands.oracle.profileId is unset, and asserts the tool result is an instructive error (no external LLM call). This guards the settings plumbing + tool registration without requiring oracle credentials.
acceptance_criteria:
- New E2E test covers ask_oracle tool call when openhands.oracle.profileId is unset.
- Test asserts the returned tool result contains an instructive configuration error message.
- Test is hermetic in CI (no external LLM/profile required).

title: Output channel: production should be quiet by default
description:
Current behavior: Debug JSON channel and dev-bridge file logging are correctly gated to Development/Test or the openhands.devBridge.enabled setting. However, we always create and show the main 'OpenHands' Output channel at activation and write informational lines in production. This can feel noisy for end users.

Proposal:
- Do not auto-show the 'OpenHands' Output channel in Production mode.
- Gate routine info logs behind a verbosity setting (default: minimal); keep warnings/errors visible.
- Keep the debug JSON channel and dev-bridge logs as-is for dev/debug.
- Add a quick command to toggle verbose/debug logging.

Rationale: Preserve rich diagnostics in dev, reduce distraction for users in production.
acceptance_criteria:
- In Production mode (ExtensionMode.Production) with openhands.devBridge.enabled=false and agent.debug unset, the extension does not auto-open the 'OpenHands' Output channel at activation.
- In that state, routine informational lines are suppressed; only warnings/errors or critical status (e.g., connection failures) appear in the Output channel.
- A user-facing setting exists to enable verbose output (boolean or verbosity enum), defaulting to minimal.
- A command exists to quickly toggle verbose/debug logging on/off.
- Documentation updated to describe the logging behavior differences between dev/debug and production.

title: Webview UX: queued user message indicator during agent run
description:
Problem
When the agent is running and a user sends a message, that message effectively "disappears" from the UI until the agent finishes the turn, then it reappears. This is confusing because it looks like the message was lost.

Proposal
Add a tiny, unobtrusive queued-message indicator (badge) that shows the number of user messages waiting to be processed by the agent.

Design notes
- Location: top-right corner of the prompt input box (or adjacent to the Send button), consistent with the small superscript badges used on Tools/Skills controls.
- Behavior:
  - Hidden when the queue size is 0.
  - Shows a small badge (e.g., a circle with a number) when >= 1 queued messages.
  - Updates in real time as messages are queued and then consumed by the agent.
  - Tooltip: "Queued messages waiting for the agent to finish the current turn".
- Accessibility: ensure screen readers announce the badge and its changes (aria-live polite).
- Telemetry/Debug (optional): log transitions to Output channel only in dev/debug mode.

Implementation sketch
- Webview state: track a  count in the Conversation UI state. Increment on user send when agent is busy; decrement on each turn start/consume.
- Message flow: reuse existing host/webview messages; no new network calls.
- Styling: match existing badge UI (Tools/Skills superscript style).

Out of scope
- Changing how queued messages are rendered in the transcript (future enhancement could show a ghost/placeholder entry).

Risks & mitigations
- Risk: badge could become noisy for rapid multi-queue. Mitigate with minimal animation and text-only badge.
- Risk: inaccurate count if events drop. Mitigate by syncing with authoritative host-side queue length if available (fallback to webview-only count).
acceptance_criteria:
- When agent is running and a user sends N messages, a badge appears showing N queued messages.
- The badge updates as messages are consumed; hides when count returns to 0.
- Badge placement is near the input box (or send button), styled like existing superscripts.
- Accessible: screen readers announce the badge and changes; color contrast meets guidelines.
- No change to message transport; only UI/UX feedback is added.

title: User message suffix: <environment information> with active file and open editors (local mode)
description:
Goal: Ensure every user prompt (start of a new local conversation and subsequent messages) is augmented with an additional suffix that conveys environment context.

Suffix format (exact tag name):
<environment information>
Active editor: <name or path>
Open editors:
- <name or path>
- ...
</environment information>

Rules:
- Local mode only (remote optional in follow-up).
- Active editor: include if any; otherwise omit line or say 'none'.
- Open editors: include all visible text editors.
- For files inside the current workspace folder: show only the file name (basename), not absolute or relative path.
- For files outside the workspace folder: show absolute paths.
- If multiple workspace files share the same basename, append a short disambiguation (e.g., 'name — sub/dir').

Verification (first):
- Audit current code: extension sets systemMessageSuffix with active file (Currently
acceptance_criteria:
- In local mode, each user message (including the first of a new conversation) includes a suffix exactly delimited by <environment information> ... </environment information>.
- Suffix lists: (a) the active editor (or none) and (b) the open editors.
- Workspace files are shown by basename only; files outside the workspace are shown by absolute path; duplicate basenames are disambiguated.
- Remote mode behavior is unchanged.
- Unit tests cover formatter and message augmentation; optional E2E validates end-to-end.

title: Remote mode: Server selector shows 'No Server' while connected
description:
Observed after successful HAL teleport to remote runtime:

- Status pill shows Connected.
- Server dropdown at top-left shows "No Server".
- Expected: it should show the active server label (first server in list), e.g. label "local-remote" for http://localhost:3000.

Screenshot: 2026-01-13 02:24 (teleport success).

Hypothesis:
- currentServerUrl/currentServerLabel state is not updated on teleport success / remote connect, or the server list UI is not using the same source of truth as the active remote connection.
acceptance_criteria:
- Investigate how the server selector derives its display value in remote mode.
- Ensure that when connected to a remote server, the dropdown shows the active server label/url (e.g. local-remote / http://127.0.0.1:3000) instead of "No Server".
- Add/adjust a webview test if feasible.
